behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is mainly a cross-module handoff behavior (ingest/parsing/correlation), not just one local function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level because the text only says grouping by trace ID across services, not real external API/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real ingestion + correlation modules together on mixed-service JSON fixtures; assert all entries sharing a trace ID end up in one group independent of service name.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on interaction between correlation/timeline ordering and skew-handling rules across service-originated events. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text is about ordering logic and skew tolerance, not external contract via real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed multi-service correlated events with controlled timestamp offsets (including ±500ms skew) through the real timeline builder; assert resulting order matches skew-correction expectations.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks percentile computation + baseline comparison + report flag propagation across modules. REAL-INFRA: no explicit real external API/OS/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly includes being flagged in the anomaly report (module handoff), not only local math.
behaviors.test_description: Process synthetic latency distributions through the full analyzer and reporting path; verify when p99 exceeds 3x baseline, the anomaly report includes the spike flag with the correct metric context.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: resilience depends on parser error handling, pipeline continuation, and reporting count consistency across components. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because no explicit external-contract round-trip or real infra trigger is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a mixed stream of valid and malformed JSON entries through real parse->process->report modules; assert pipeline completes, malformed entries are skipped, and reported skipped-count matches malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: throughput, backpressure, scheduler timing, and queue behavior under burst load are runtime/OS-sensitive; in-process mocks can hide drops or lag. This behavior requires proving externally visible side effects under real load (all entries accepted and processed without backlog growth). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the tool as a real process under load generation at 10,000 entries/sec, then round-trip verify input count equals processed/output count and backlog/latency stays within ‘not falling behind’ threshold over the burst window.
